title: Monitoring Tracker
subtitle: A living doc shape for topics that don't end — track named voices, empirical predictions, and numeric indicators every check-in, with each period visible as a delta.
scope: One topic or domain, tracked over time. Named experts whose stances evolve; predictions whose resolution accumulates evidence; indicators whose values print on a cadence; a consensus map where movement between periods is the signal.
sourceCoverage: Template only; replace placeholder experts, predictions, indicators, and sources with real material from the topic you're tracking.

templateMeta.useWhen: Use this when you want to watch a live debate, a research frontier, a market, or any topic whose state shifts with new evidence — and you want each check-in to land as an inspectable delta rather than a fresh write-up.
growsInto: More experts, predictions, or indicators as the topic widens
Companion docs per sub-topic when the scope grows past one page
templateMeta.operatingThesis: The doc is bounded by a topic, not by an outcome. Each monitoring period is a check-in whose changes are attributable to that period; the doc accumulates over time rather than finishing.
keepDistinct: which period a card was last updated in
current stance vs. evolution since last period
forecast vs. latest value vs. trend
current position on the map vs. prior-period position
sources added in this period vs. prior periods

Preserve the dated sources consumed during the current monitoring period."] decision_record["decision-record
Summarize what shifted, resolved, and matters for the next period."] citation_feed -- "informs" --> expert_stance_track citation_feed -- "informs" --> indicator_trace expert_stance_track -- "positioned-on" --> position_cluster_map indicator_trace -- "resolves" --> proof_ladder decision_record -- "summarizes" --> expert_stance_track

semanticDefinition.objectiveRole: Track an evolving topic across periods by separating voices, predictions, indicators, map movement, sources, and period notes.
semanticDefinition.templateSubtitle: A living doc shape for topics that don't end — track named voices, empirical predictions, and numeric indicators every check-in, with each period visible as a delta.
semanticDefinition.templateScope: One topic or domain, tracked over time. Named experts whose stances evolve; predictions whose resolution accumulates evidence; indicators whose values print on a cadence; a consensus map where movement between periods is the signal.
semanticDefinition.templateObjective: Track the evolution of <topic> as evidence accumulates and named positions shift, so any reader at any period check-in sees current stance, live prediction resolutions, latest indicator values, and what moved since the prior period — without re-reading the underlying sources.
semanticDefinition.templateSuccessCondition: A reader opening the doc at the end of any monitoring period knows (1) each expert's current stance and how it shifted, (2) which predictions moved toward resolution, (3) the latest indicator values with dated sources, and (4) where the consensus has tightened or widened on the map.

sections.title: Tracked voices
sections.role: Track named voices and how their stances evolve over monitoring periods.
sections.rationale: The named voices whose public-intellectual stance evolves over time. One card per voice.

sections.title: Empirical predictions
sections.role: Track what-to-watch claims as evidence accumulates.
sections.rationale: The 'what to watch' claims rendered as a proof ladder. Each rung resolves as evidence accumulates across periods.

sections.title: Indicator dashboard
sections.role: Track numeric indicators and their direction across periods.
sections.rationale: Numeric indicators tracked across periods. Latest value is text; trend is categorical (rising/stable/falling/resolved).

sections.title: Consensus & divergence map
sections.role: Map current and prior positions so movement between periods is visible.
sections.rationale: Two-axis placement of tracked voices. Movement between monitoring periods is the primary signal. Prior-period position is preserved on each card.

sections.title: Sources this period
sections.role: Preserve the dated sources consumed during the current monitoring period.
sections.rationale: Period-grouped citation feed. Each source added in the current period lives here; retroactive additions to prior periods are allowed but should be flagged in notes.

sections.title: Period notes
sections.role: Summarize what shifted, resolved, and matters for the next period.
sections.rationale: One card per monitoring period summarising what shifted, what resolved, and what matters for next.

citation-feed.name: Citation Feed
citation-feed.description: Dated source entries (papers, interviews, op-eds) grouped by monitoring period. Used when the doc accumulates reference material over time with period attribution.
citation-feed.structuralContract: One-column card grid. Each card is one citation with title, author, venue, publication date, and url. Carries a citation-state status and a lastUpdatedInPeriod marker so period-grouping renders. Use for monitoring docs; not for flat reference lists.

promptGuidance.operatingThesis: Each card is one dated source added in a specific monitoring period; grouping by period is what makes the feed useful.
keepDistinct: the period the citation was added in
inspect: Check the publication date belongs to the current period before adding to this period's group.
update: Retroactive additions to a prior period are allowed but flag with a note.
Mark retracted sources explicitly; do not delete history.
avoid: Do not add sources without a publication date.
Do not duplicate a source that already exists in a prior period.

decision-record.name: Decision Record
decision-record.description: A compact set of decisions or operating choices that should be treated as authoritative ground truth.
decision-record.structuralContract: One-column card grid of page-status items, usually ground-truth or reference. Use when the section captures decisions, operating models, or declared truths rather than implementation work.

promptGuidance.operatingThesis: Treat decisions as authoritative ground truth or reference statements, not implementation work.
inspect: Check whether the decision is still current before marking it ground-truth.
update: Keep decision entries compact and authoritative.
avoid: Do not mix factual findings, verification rungs, or component status into decisions.

aiActions.name: Check if still current
aiActions.description: Read recent commits, tickets, and sessions for signals that a ground-truth decision has been implicitly overridden. Propose softening to reference or opening a challenge card.

expert-stance-track.name: Expert Stance Track
expert-stance-track.description: Sustained public-intellectual stance across many works, tracked across monitoring periods as evidence accumulates and positions evolve.
expert-stance-track.structuralContract: One-column card grid. Each card names an expert by name + affiliation and carries current stance, core view, evolution since the last monitoring period, and latest source references. Use this when tracking a set of named voices whose positions shift over time.
notFor: one-off forum or PR comments (use maintainer-stance)
stance on a single issue thread

promptGuidance.operatingThesis: Treat each card as a sustained public-intellectual stance that evolves over monitoring periods, not a one-off comment.
inspect: Check what the expert has published in the current monitoring period before changing their stance.
update: When a stance softens, retracts, or reinforces, record it as a status transition plus an evolution note that cites the triggering source.
Preserve prior-period evolution notes as history; append rather than overwrite.
avoid: Do not invent stance shifts without a dated source from the current period.
Do not use this for anonymous or aggregated sentiment.

indicator-trace.name: Indicator Trace
indicator-trace.description: Tracked metric with dated latest observation, forecast, and delta versus the prior monitoring period. Used when the doc is watching a set of empirical indicators over time.
indicator-trace.structuralContract: Two-column card grid. Each card is one tracked indicator with a latest value (as text), an as-of date, a forecast, a delta versus the last period, and a categorical trend (rising / stable / falling / resolved). Use for dashboards of empirical signals; not for categorical deliverables.

promptGuidance.operatingThesis: Each card is a numeric indicator whose value is dated and whose direction since the last period is what matters most.
keepDistinct: latest value with an as-of date
inspect: Confirm the latest value has a source with a publication date.
Compare the value against the last-period value before setting deltaVsLastPeriod.
update: Update latestValue and asOf together; never update one without the other.
Set trend only when the delta supports it.
avoid: Do not flip trend on a single data point if the series is noisy.
Do not substitute categorical status for numeric value.

indicator-trace.icon: <path opacity='.28' d='M4 5h16v14H4z'/><path d='M6 17l4-5 3 3 5-7' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.8' stroke-linecap='round' stroke-linejoin='round'/>

position-cluster-map.name: Position Cluster Map
position-cluster-map.description: Two-axis layout where named thinkers or stakeholders are placed to visualise consensus and divergence. Movement between monitoring periods is the primary signal.
position-cluster-map.structuralContract: Two-column card grid. Each card is one thinker with an x-axis value, a y-axis value, and an optional prior-period position for comparison. Uses stance-status to reflect movement direction. Use for consensus/divergence maps; not for generic ranking or scorecards.

promptGuidance.operatingThesis: The map's value is showing movement between periods, not a static snapshot of current positions.
inspect: Re-derive coordinates from the expert-stance-track cards in the same doc before updating; do not fabricate movement.
update: When a thinker moves on an axis, preserve the prior position so the delta is inspectable.
Update the movement status to reflect the direction of the shift.
avoid: Do not invent coordinates without evidence in the companion stance cards.
Do not remove prior positions; they are the historical record.

position-cluster-map.icon: <path opacity='.22' d='M4 4h16v16H4z'/><path d='M4 12h16M12 4v16' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.2'/><circle cx='8' cy='9' r='1.5'/><circle cx='15' cy='14' r='1.5'/><circle cx='10' cy='17' r='1.5'/>

proof-ladder.name: Proof Ladder
proof-ladder.description: An ordered ladder of verification steps where each rung increases proof strength toward operational truth.
proof-ladder.structuralContract: Two-column card grid of probe-status items, typically named as levels or rungs. Use when the section expresses staged proof escalation rather than a flat set of checks.

promptGuidance.operatingThesis: Treat proof as staged escalation where each rung increases confidence toward operational truth.
inspect: Check whether evidence actually satisfies the rung before advancing status.
update: Preserve ladder ordering and make weaker or missing rungs explicit.
avoid: Do not flatten proof levels into unordered checks or decisions.

aiActions.name: Check monotonic invariant
aiActions.description: Confirm every rung below this one is ready. If not, flag the inversion — this rung cannot legitimately claim ready state.

aiProfiles.name: Proof State Brief
aiProfiles.description: Summarize what the ladder currently proves and where the proof boundary sits.

aiProfiles.name: Weakest Open Rung
aiProfiles.description: Identify the first unsatisfied rung and the missing evidence for closure.

aiProfiles.name: Next Stronger Proof
aiProfiles.description: Describe the next evidence shape that would strengthen the ladder without redesigning it.

relationships.rationale: Expert stance updates should be grounded in sources read during the monitoring period.
Indicator values need dated source backing so period movement can be audited.
The consensus map should place tracked voices and preserve their movement between periods.
Predictions move toward resolution when indicators change or new values arrive.
Period notes should summarize stance shifts, prediction movement, indicator changes, and map movement.

stageSignals.question: Which named voices define this monitoring topic?

stageSignals.when: expert stances exist but citation-feed has no sources
stageSignals.question: Which dated source supports the current stance update?

stageSignals.when: expert stances exist but position-cluster-map is empty
stageSignals.question: Where do the tracked voices sit on the consensus map this period?

stageSignals.when: indicators exist but proof-ladder has no prediction rungs
stageSignals.question: Which prediction did the indicator value move toward resolution?

stageSignals.when: voices, predictions, indicators, map, sources, and period notes are populated
stageSignals.question: Can a reader understand what shifted this period without rereading the sources?

validOperations.label: Add tracked voice
validOperations.description: Add a named expert or voice with current stance and evolution.

validOperations.label: Add period source
validOperations.description: Add a dated source consumed during the current monitoring period.

validOperations.label: Update position map
validOperations.description: Add or update the consensus/divergence map position for a tracked voice.

validOperations.label: Record indicator value
validOperations.description: Add or update a dated numeric indicator value and trend.

validOperations.label: Write period note
validOperations.description: Record what shifted, resolved, and matters for the next monitoring period.

objective: Track the evolution of <topic> as evidence accumulates and named positions shift, so any reader at any period check-in sees current stance, live prediction resolutions, latest indicator values, and what moved since the prior period — without re-reading the underlying sources.
successCondition: A reader opening the doc at the end of any monitoring period knows (1) each expert's current stance and how it shifted, (2) which predictions moved toward resolution, (3) the latest indicator values with dated sources, and (4) where the consensus has tightened or widened on the map.

objectiveFacets.name: Current positions accurate
objectiveFacets.description: Each tracked expert's stance reflects their most recent published work in the current period.

objectiveFacets.name: Predictions tracked
objectiveFacets.description: Every prediction has a live resolution status with evidence accumulating.

objectiveFacets.name: Indicators current
objectiveFacets.description: Numerical indicators match the latest prints, each with an as-of date and a source.

objectiveFacets.name: Divergence map current
objectiveFacets.description: The consensus/divergence axes reflect current positions; prior-period positions preserved.

objectiveFacets.name: Period attribution
objectiveFacets.description: Every card update is attributable to a named monitoring period.

periods.summary: Baseline — replace with a one-sentence summary of what this first period captured.

invariants.name: Expert stance must cite period source
invariants.statement: Every expert-position card must cite at least one source dated in the current or prior period. No unchanged snapshots without evidence.

invariants.name: Every prediction carries a resolution status
invariants.statement: Every prediction rung must carry a resolution status (planned / partial / ready / blocked) with rationale.

invariants.name: Every indicator is dated
invariants.statement: Every indicator card must have a latestValue with an as-of date and a source.

invariants.name: Every source has a publication date
invariants.statement: Every citation card must have a publishedAt field.

invariants.name: Period note exists when period closes
invariants.statement: When the current monitoring period has closed, the period note for that period must exist.

invariants.name: Divergence map covers all tracked voices
invariants.statement: The divergence map must include position cards for every voice in the experts section.

callouts.title: How to use this template
items: Replace <topic> in the objective with the thing you're tracking (a debate, a market, a research frontier).
Replace the placeholder experts with the named voices you actually follow. One card per voice.
Turn each 'what to watch' question you care about into a prediction rung. Leave status as `planned` until evidence lands.
Pick 3–6 indicators you can realistically refresh each period. Numeric value goes in latestValue as text; direction goes in trend.
The divergence map is optional — use it when consensus vs. divergence across voices is a first-class question.
The citation feed groups by period via each card's lastUpdatedInPeriod. Retroactive additions to prior periods are allowed but should be flagged in notes.
Every check-in: add a new entry to periods[], update the cards that moved (setting lastUpdatedInPeriod on each), draft a period note in the period-notes section.

sections.title: Tracked voices
sections.rationale: The named voices whose public-intellectual stance evolves over time. One card per voice.

data.evolutionSinceLastPeriod: Baseline — P1 is the initial capture.
whatTheyEmphasise: <First point they emphasise, with a fact or number if available>

notes.text: <Supporting source or data point with date>

data.name: <Second expert>
data.evolutionSinceLastPeriod: Baseline — P1 is the initial capture.

data.name: <Expert name>

data.axisY: <position on axis Y, e.g. 'market-led' or a phrase>

sections.title: Sources this period
sections.rationale: Period-grouped citation feed. Each source added in the current period lives here; retroactive additions to prior periods are allowed but should be flagged in notes.
